seo_description: Current time in Tirana, Albania. Live CET/CEST clock — UTC+1 standard, UTC+2 summer. Tirana time vs London, Rome, Athens, New York.

faq.question: What time is it in Tirana right now?
faq.answer: Tirana operates on Central European Time (CET/UTC+1) during winter and Central European Summer Time (CEST/UTC+2) during summer. Albania's capital follows the same EU-aligned DST schedule as neighboring Balkan countries. To see the exact current time in Tirana with live seconds, use our real-time clock above.

faq.question: What time zone is Tirana in?
faq.answer: Tirana is in the Central European Time zone (CET/UTC+1 in winter, CEST/UTC+2 in summer). Albania shares this timezone with Italy, Serbia, North Macedonia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Montenegro, and Kosovo. Clocks advance one hour on the last Sunday in March and fall back on the last Sunday in October, following the standard EU Daylight Saving Time schedule.

faq.question: Does Tirana observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: Yes. Tirana and all of Albania observe Daylight Saving Time following the EU schedule. Clocks spring forward one hour on the last Sunday in March at 2:00 AM CET (becoming 3:00 AM CEST/UTC+2), and fall back one hour on the last Sunday in October at 3:00 AM CEST (becoming 2:00 AM CET/UTC+1). Although Albania is an EU candidate state rather than a full member, it aligns its DST dates with European Union rules.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tirana and London?
faq.answer: Tirana is 1 hour ahead of London during UK winter (GMT/UTC+0), and 1 hour ahead during British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) — because both cities advance their clocks on the same schedule. The 1-hour gap remains constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in London, it is 10:00 AM in Tirana.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tirana and New York?
faq.answer: Tirana is 6 hours ahead of New York (EST/UTC-5) during winter, and 6 hours ahead during EDT as well, since both regions observe DST on roughly the same schedule. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 3:00 PM in Tirana. The morning New York window (9:00 AM–1:00 PM EST) aligns with Tirana's afternoon (3:00 PM–7:00 PM CET).

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tirana and Paris?
faq.answer: Tirana and Paris share the same time zone — both are on CET (UTC+1) in winter and CEST (UTC+2) in summer. There is no time difference between Tirana and Paris, Rome, Berlin, Vienna, or any other CET/CEST city. Scheduling calls between Tirana and Western European capitals requires no time zone adjustment.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Tirana and Dubai?
faq.answer: Dubai (GST/UTC+4) is 3 hours ahead of Tirana during winter (CET/UTC+1) and 2 hours ahead during European summer (CEST/UTC+2). When it is 9:00 AM in Tirana, it is 12:00 PM in Dubai in winter, and 11:00 AM in summer. Albania has a growing economic relationship with Gulf states, particularly through diaspora investment.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Tirana?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Tirana run 8:00 AM–5:00 PM CET/CEST Monday through Friday. Government offices and public institutions typically open 8:00 AM–4:00 PM. Banks operate 9:00 AM–3:30 PM on weekdays. Retail shops in Tirana's Blloku district and Rruga e Kavajës stay open until 8:00 PM or later. The city has a vibrant cafe culture, with most restaurants open 8:00 AM–11:00 PM or midnight.

content_blocks.title: Tirana Time Zone — CET/CEST, Balkan Standard
content_blocks.content: Tirana, Albania's capital, follows Central European Time (CET/UTC+1) in winter and Central European Summer Time (CEST/UTC+2) from late March to late October. This places Tirana in sync with Rome, Belgrade, Sarajevo, Skopje, and Pristina — all sharing the CET/CEST pattern across the western Balkans. Albania is 1 hour ahead of London year-round (the UK also shifts its clocks at a similar time), 6 hours ahead of New York (EST), and 3 hours behind Dubai. Greece and Turkey, Tirana's eastern neighbors, are on EET/EEST (UTC+2/+3), making them 1 hour ahead of Tirana in winter. DST in Albania follows the EU schedule: clocks advance on the last Sunday in March and retreat on the last Sunday in October.

content_blocks.title: Tirana: Albania's Growing Capital and Balkans Gateway
content_blocks.content: Tirana is the political, economic, and cultural heart of Albania, home to nearly 900,000 people and growing rapidly. Albania is a NATO member since 2009 and an official EU candidate country since 2014, with accession negotiations advancing. The city has undergone a dramatic transformation since the 1990s, shifting from isolation to a vibrant Balkan hub. Tourism has surged on the back of the Albanian Riviera (Sarandë, Ksamil) and Tirana's own colorful street art and cafe culture. The 'Blloku' district — once reserved for Communist Party elite — is now the city's hippest neighborhood. Key industries include construction, retail trade, telecoms, and financial services. Tirana is also the gateway to the Western Balkans for logistics firms, with road links to Kosovo, North Macedonia, Montenegro, and the Adriatic port of Durrës.

content_blocks.title: Scheduling and Calling Tirana Internationally
content_blocks.content: Tirana's CET/CEST alignment makes it straightforward to coordinate with Western European partners — no offset adjustment needed for calls with Rome, Berlin, or Paris. From London (GMT), Tirana is always 1 hour ahead, so UK callers should add an hour when scheduling. From New York (EST), Tirana is 6 hours ahead in winter: a 9:00 AM New York call reaches Tirana at 3:00 PM CET, well within business hours. From Dubai (GST/UTC+4), Tirana is 3 hours behind in winter, so Dubai callers reaching out at 12:00 PM reach Tirana at 9:00 AM — ideal for morning meetings. From Tokyo (JST/UTC+9), Tirana is 8 hours behind in winter, making early Tokyo mornings the best window for same-day Tirana afternoon contact. Albania's country code is +355 and Tirana's area code is 4.

timezone_facts.full_name: Central European Time / Central European Summer Time
timezone_facts.dst_start: Last Sunday in March at 2:00 AM CET
timezone_facts.dst_end: Last Sunday in October at 3:00 AM CEST
